factors.name: Japan GDP beat
factors.summary: Japan Q1 GDP annualized 2.1% vs 1.7% expected, beating on growth. Positive for risk sentiment and USD weakness, but modest direct impact on NAS100.

factors.name: Iran geopolitical relief
factors.summary: Trump postponed scheduled Iran strike; Saudi/UAE/Qatar seeking nuclear deal. Eases oil risk and inflation expectations, reducing near-term recession probability (Goldman cut to 25% from 30%).

factors.name: Fed policy shift to hawkish (Warsh transition)
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh confirmed as Fed chair; MUFG expects dollar extension and higher real yields. Goolsbee reinforced inflation 'front of mind' messaging. Markets pricing 50% chance of rate hike by year-end.

factors.name: Real yields and long-duration sensitivity
factors.summary: Warsh hawkishness signals sticky real yields; 10Y Treasury volatility and bond rout warnings from Morgan Stanley. Long-duration tech (NDX-heavy) vulnerable if real yields stay elevated or rise further.

factors.name: USD strength
factors.summary: Dollar posted best week since Iran conflict (up 1.4%) on hawkish Fed + hot inflation data. NDX has heavy international revenue exposure; stronger USD headwind for earnings translation.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley equity correction warning
factors.summary: Morgan Stanley warns bond rout could trigger meaningful correction if volatility persists, though bank raises S&P 500 target to 8,300. Suggests near-term chop risk even with bullish longer-term view.

factors.name: Inflation expectations (RBA, Japan GDP deflator)
factors.summary: RBA's Hunter warns of elevated risk of inflation expectations drifting higher from oil pass-through; Japan GDP deflator running 3.4% y/y (hot). Hawkish central bank bias emerging globally.

factors.name: Options positioning on NVDA / tech earnings
factors.summary: NVDA call volume outpaced puts >2x, but more calls traded at bid/below (likely sold, not bought). Positioning mixed; earnings anxiety evident ahead of Nvidia results.

factors.name: Price action: 4h +0.29%, 24h -0.17%
factors.summary: Minimal intraday move; mixed overnight action (Dow +, S&P/Nadaq -). Rebound off session lows on Iran news, but no conviction breakout. Suggests market still digesting conflicting forces.

factors.name: Tech mega-cap sentiment mixed
factors.summary: ServiceNow +1yr best day (software optimism), but Seagate weakness, Nvidia questions, and Musk/OpenAI verdict overhang. No consensus direction in semis or mega-cap growth.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between two opposing macro headwinds: (1) Hawkish Fed transition (Warsh) + sticky real yields + strong dollar = bearish for long-duration growth multiples; (2) Iran geopolitical relief + Japan growth beat + recession odds falling = bullish risk sentiment. The index rebounded off session lows on Iran pause news, but lacks conviction (only +0.29% in 4h, -0.17% on 24h). Morgan Stanley warns of correction risk if bond volatility persists, signaling chop ahead of major tech earnings. Real yields, not nominal yields, are the critical variable for NDX valuation—and Warsh signals they'll stay elevated.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings (TBD, likely late May — critical for semis & AI capex narrative)
MSFT/GOOGL/AAPL earnings updates
FOMC minutes & upcoming rate decision (re-rate expectations on Warsh commentary)
CPI/Core CPI data (next scheduled print ~mid-June)
Treasury yield moves (track 10Y & TIPS closely for real yield regime)
Google I/O AI showcase (May 20 — potential AI capex/cloud demand signaling)
Meta restructuring details (May 20 — headcount/efficiency pivot)
US-Iran nuclear deal outcome (ongoing, impacts oil, inflation expectations)
screening.risk_warning: If Iran nuclear deal breaks down and oil spikes >$150/bbl, geopolitical premium reinstates and inflation expectations rise sharply—crushing long-duration equities and overriding Fed hawkishness relief. Conversely, if Warsh signals are misread as dovish by markets or CPI cools sharply, real yields could fall and NDX could re-rate higher. Major tech earnings disappointments or cloud capex guidance cuts would flip bias decisively bearish regardless of macro backdrop.
screening.entry_guidance: Market in chop zone (move underway but inconclusive). Avoid chasing; wait for clearer directional conviction on next 10Y yield move (track 4h close) or hold for NVDA/MSFT earnings catalyst. If resistance at 29100+ breaks on volume, re-assess bullish. If support 28800 breaks, confirm bear bias and short into 28600–28400 zone.

factors.name: US Treasury Yields / Real Yields
factors.summary: 10Y yields flirting with 2007 highs; bond rout persists as inflation concerns mount (AllianzGI warns markets may be overpricing inflation). Rising real yields compress long-duration tech multiples, headwind for NQ components.

factors.name: Fed Policy / Warsh Appointment
factors.summary: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh signals hawkish shift on inflation; rate-hike expectations rising. MUFG notes dollar posted best week since Iran conflict partly on Warsh hawkish signal. Equity-negative dynamic.

factors.name: Inflation Data & Pass-Through Risk
factors.summary: RBA Assistant Governor Hunter warns elevated inflation expectations risk from Middle East energy shock; producer price pressures building in NZ/Australia. No fresh CPI/PCE yet, but expectations shifting higher.

factors.name: NVDA Earnings (Wed 20 May)
factors.summary: InvestingLive reports NVDA has moderately bearish event-risk bias (-3.4/+10 score); stock already made large move into earnings, tested resistance near $236.54 and failed to break. Earnings-day volatility risk.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran / Middle East)
factors.summary: Trump postponed Iran strike at Gulf leaders' request, reducing immediate tail risk. However, uncertainty remains 'very binary' per Bloomberg; oil fell 2% but energy volatility persists, keeping risk sentiment fragile.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD Index holds gains above 99.00; dollar posted strongest week in months on Warsh hawkish signal + inflation concerns. Strong dollar headwind for NQ international revenue exposure (~40% of mega-cap earnings from overseas).

factors.name: Tech Sentiment / Breadth
factors.summary: Korean stocks down 5% as chip slump fuels overheating concerns; weak breadth in high-flying names. Analyst caution on AI capex sustainability vs. current valuations.

factors.name: China Macro / Demand
factors.summary: Iron ore fell to 2-week low on China steel demand concerns; China resumed foreign inflows post-Iran outflow, but momentum fragile. Putin heading to Beijing for energy talks; geopolitical jostling unclear for tech supply chains.

factors.name: Price Action Context
factors.summary: NAS100 +0.29% in 4h but -0.17% in 24h; micro-bounce into earnings. Move is marginal, no strong directional conviction yet. Prior read (2026-05-19 00:05) was NEUTRAL 5/10.

factors.name: Morgan Stanley Correction Warning
factors.summary: MS warns equities face first meaningful correction since late March if bond volatility persists; though MS itself raises S&P 500 target to 8300. Mixed signal but correction risk noted.

screening.narrative: NAS100 is caught between a tightening Fed narrative (Warsh hawkish shift, rising real yields, inflation expectations drifting higher) and a mixed geopolitical backstop (Iran strike postponed, but energy volatility lingers). The massive bond rout and yield surge—especially in real rates—are direct headwinds for long-duration growth multiples. NVDA's moderately bearish earnings setup (large move into event, failed breakout near $236.54) adds single-name tail risk to the index 24h out. Sentiment is fragile; breadth concerns in tech (Korean chip stocks down 5%) suggest conviction is fading into the event cycle.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings (Wed 20 May 2026) — single biggest catalyst; any miss or soft guidance 2-3% NQ swing risk
FOMC meeting / Fed communications — Warsh's first actions & tone-setting highly watched
US inflation data (CPI/PCE) — any hot print will spike yields further, crush growth multiples
MSFT, GOOGL, AAPL earnings (late May)
10Y Treasury auction, real yield trends
Iran escalation / geopolitical resolution (oil/dollar feedback loops)
screening.risk_warning: If NVDA beats and provides strong forward guidance, or if Fed communication softens hawkish tone, NQ could rally 1-2% quickly. Conversely, if real yields spike further (e.g., CPI hot) or NVDA disappoints, -2 to -3% correction likely. Bond market stability is the linchpin; sustained >4.50% 10Y with climbing real yields = extended downside.
screening.entry_guidance: Market is pricing NVDA event risk; wait for post-earnings retest or a pullback to 28,800–28,900 support before considering longs. Shorts on fresh break below 28,800 with conviction only if yields climb >4.50% and stay there. Earnings day (Wed) = elevated chop; prefer entry after volatility settles.

factors.name: US Treasury Yield Shock
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yields hit highest since 1999 per Bloomberg; bond market selloff ongoing as traders eye persistent inflation. Long-duration tech multiples under pressure from elevated real yields.

factors.name: Inflation Expectations Rising
factors.summary: Iran war energy shock driving upward pressure on inflation per OECD; UK energy price cap surging 13% this summer; global finance chiefs bracing for enduring consumer-price shock per Bloomberg.

factors.name: Fed Rate Cut Expectations Pushed Back
factors.summary: BNY strategists abandoned call for two 2026 rate cuts, citing persistent Strait of Hormuz disruption and stronger-than-expected labor market. Market now contemplating potential Fed hike by mid-2027 per Commerzbank.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk / Iran-US Tensions
factors.summary: Trump paused planned Iran strike after Middle East leader requests, easing immediate oil shock and risk-off sentiment. However, negotiations remain stalled and Hormuz closure risks persist—66% of fund managers expect Hormuz bottleneck to end in next few months per BofA survey.

factors.name: Oil Price Volatility & Supply Concerns
factors.summary: Oil retreated ~2% to $109.8/bbl after Trump strike pause, but supply concerns remain fragmented. Indonesia nickel output cuts adding commodity inflation signals.

factors.name: Dollar Strength & Hawkish Fed Narrative
factors.summary: USD recovering strongly; Barclays noting potential for 'sizable rally' this week on inflation concerns and Fed rate-hike bets. DXY strength headwind for tech with international revenue exposure, but supports risk-off tone.

factors.name: Tech Sector Earnings Pressure
factors.summary: S&P 500 showed tech underperformance and defensive rotation; NVDA earnings scheduled Wed 20 May with moderately bearish event-risk bias (-3.4/+10 prediction score per analysis). Hyperscaler capex commentary crucial but flagged as potential headwind given higher rates.

factors.name: AI Supply Chain Stress
factors.summary: Iran war exposing weak spots in AI chip supply chain; costs rising and access to key materials at risk per Bloomberg. Semiconductor sector scrambling to shore up supply.

factors.name: Fund Manager Risk Appetite & Positioning
factors.summary: BofA survey shows record equity allocations in May, but only 4% of fund managers see hard landing; cash levels dropped to 3.9% (biggest monthly drop since Feb 2024). Second wave of inflation flagged as biggest tail risk by 40% of respondents.

factors.name: Recent Price Action Context
factors.summary: NAS100 up +0.29% in 4h but down -0.17% in 24h. Minimal momentum; consolidating near recent highs after late-session tech recovery Monday. Move is in-progress but not extended—suggests Phase 2 caution.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq faces a tug-of-war between tech earnings momentum (NVDA tomorrow) and macro headwinds from rising real yields, inflation expectations, and delayed Fed rate-cut timelines. The Iran war has shifted the risk narrative from geopolitical shock (initial risk-off) to persistent energy inflation and higher-for-longer rates—a structural drag on long-duration growth stocks. Treasury yields at 1999 highs and hawkish Fed repricing are pressuring valuation multiples, while AI supply-chain disruptions add sector-specific risk. Fund manager positioning remains elevated, suggesting late-cycle froth, but earnings upside (hyperscaler capex, cloud demand) could provide a temporary relief bounce into NVDA earnings Wed.
catalysts_ahead: NVDA earnings Wed 20 May (high-impact AI demand signal)
Canada CPI data Tue 19 May (inflation print)
FOMC meeting outcomes and Fed communications
US CPI/PCE inflation surprises (dates TBD in next 1-2 weeks)
Tech mega-cap earnings: MSFT, AAPL, GOOGL, AMZN (guidance on capex/cloud growth)
US-Iran escalation risk (geopolitical binary that could flip risk sentiment)
screening.risk_warning: If NVDA and cloud hyperscalers deliver strong capex and AI demand guidance Wed, AND Treasury yields stabilize or pull back, NAS100 could break above 29250 and extend rally. Conversely, if earnings disappoint or management signals capex slowdown due to cost inflation, combined with further Treasury yield spikes above 4.5%, NAS100 breaks below 28850 into 28600–28700. Major unknown: whether Iran-US talks result in sanctions relief (bullish for oil, commodity inflation eases) or further escalation (bearish growth, energy shock deepens).

factors.name: Treasury Yields & Real Rates
factors.summary: 10-year yields climbing above 4.6% (Bloomberg 11:31), near 2007 highs. Higher nominal and real yields compress long-duration tech multiples, directly hostile to NQ valuation.

factors.name: US Dollar Strength
factors.summary: USD higher vs major currencies (AUD +0.64%, NZD +0.49%, EUR +0.30%) as risk sentiment softens. Strong dollar headwind for mega-cap tech international revenues (~40-50% of AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL).

factors.name: Equity Futures Weakness
factors.summary: S&P 500 futures -0.4%, Nasdaq 100 futures near 29,100 (vs spot 28,994). Semis (Micron, Nvidia) extending declines per Bloomberg 11:31. Market rejecting higher yields.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran-US De-escalation)
factors.summary: Trump postpones Iran strike after Gulf allies request diplomacy (Bloomberg 10:40). Oil prices falling (~$102.75, down from highs), reducing stagflation risk and inflation hedge pressure on equities.

factors.name: AI Narrative & Capex Momentum
factors.summary: JPMorgan notes AI moving from 'hype to real execution' (11:22). Blackstone-Google $5B AI infrastructure venture (08:53), NextEra battery mega-deal (11:41), and Anthropic as #1 Disruptor (11:05) signal sustained hyperscaler demand.

factors.name: Sector Divergence & Valuation Reset
factors.summary: CoreWeave, Nebius tumble on Google-Blackstone competition (11:59). Higher yields pressure valuation multiples across mega-cap tech. Profit-taking and rotation risk evident in futures.

factors.name: Earnings Resilience (Retail Sample)
factors.summary: Home Depot beat on top & bottom lines, raised FY guidance despite higher gas prices (11:02). Consumer resilience and corporate earnings quality provide floor for equities.

factors.name: Macro Headwinds: Energy, Growth Outlook
factors.summary: BofA cuts European growth outlook on Iran war and weak data (11:59). ECB's Villeroy flags dual supply shocks (inflation & growth). Standard Chartered slashing 8,000 jobs for AI displacement.

factors.name: Prior Bias History
factors.summary: Three consecutive NEUTRAL 5/10 reads (00:05, 05:00, 08:37) with no confirmation. Current print remains flat 4h (+0.29%) and slightly negative 24h (-0.17%), consistent with consolidation.

factors.name: VIX & Volatility Regime
factors.summary: No VIX data in feed; however, futures weakness and yield jumps suggest elevated uncertainty. Likely volatility expansion near 4.6% yield threshold.

screening.narrative: NAS100 faces a critical inflection as 10-year Treasury yields spike above 4.6% (near 2007 highs), directly threatening the valuation multiples that drove the April-May rally. While de-escalation of Iran tensions and strong AI capex narrative (Blackstone-Google deal, Anthropic leadership) provide tactical bullish support, equity futures are rolling over (-0.4% ES), semiconductors are extending losses, and the USD is strengthening—all classic pre-correction signals. The market is caught between bullish AI fundamentals and bearish real rates / growth deceleration fears. Current consolidation (NEUTRAL bias for 3+ hours) suggests traders are in a holding pattern ahead of macro clarity (FOMC minutes, flash PMIs, CPI ahead).
catalysts_ahead: FOMC Minutes (likely this week) — will guide Fed pause/pivot expectations and recalibrate terminal rate
US Flash PMI (manufacturing, services) — key inflation/activity gauge to assess stagflation risk
Canada CPI (Tuesday per news) — energy price pass-through will inform BoC/broader inflation narrative
Tech Mega-Cap Earnings: AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN (weeks ahead) — guidance on capex, AI demand, macro outlook critical
NVDA earnings & cloud capex commentary — hyperscaler order trends and semiconductor demand next major inflection
US 30-year Treasury yield action — if breaks above 2007 highs (~5.1%), risk of significant equities selloff
screening.risk_warning: This bias becomes BULLISH if: (1) 10-year yields roll over below 4.4%, or (2) FOMC signals dovish pivot, or (3) major tech earnings beat with strong 2H26 guidance. Bias becomes SELL if: yields break above 4.7% sustainably, semis enter downtrend (Nvidia, TSMC weakness), or macro data confirms stagflation (hot CPI + weak PMI). Overnight gap risk remains elevated given geopolitical uncertainty and cross-asset volatility.
screening.entry_guidance: NEUTRAL skew means avoid large directional bets; wait for either a 4.5% yield test (short bias) or FOMC dovish surprise / earnings relief rally (long bias). If buying, wait for retest of 28,800-28,900 support with improved breadth (Nasdaq advancing % >50%). If shorting, wait for confirmed close above 29,200 resistance breakdown.

factors.name: Real yields & long-duration growth sensitivity
factors.summary: 30-year Treasury yield hit 5.19%, highest since before 2008 financial crisis (FXStreet 15:05). Real yields remain elevated; long-duration tech (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, NVDA) extremely sensitive to this backdrop. No Fed dovish signals in newsflow.

factors.name: Mega-cap tech earnings + single-name weakness
factors.summary: NVDA -1.48%, AMD -5.71%, INTC -4.06% on the day (Investinglive 14:46). Apple hardware chief leadership shuffle (Bloomberg 15:51) adds uncertainty. Semiconductor selloff suggests demand/margin concerns amid elevated rates.

factors.name: USD strength & DXY trajectory
factors.summary: EURUSD extended lower to 1.1593 (Investinglive 16:03), lowest since April 8. USD/CAD grinding higher despite soft CPI. BBH notes DXY likely to overshoot 100.00 range on resilient US activity. Strong dollar headwind for NQ multinational revenue.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk premium (Iran conflict)
factors.summary: Trump postponed Iran strike (oil prices edged lower per 15:24 update), easing immediate supply shock. However, ongoing Middle East tensions remain; crude volatility persists. Energy demand for AI data centers partially offsets (Bloomberg 16:05 on Williams CEO gas outlook).

factors.name: Hyperscaler capex & AI demand
factors.summary: No major cloud capex guidance or hyperscaler commentary in today's newsflow. Andrej Karpathy (Tesla/OpenAI AI exec) joins Anthropic (15:37), signaling continued talent/capital flow to AI, but no earnings surprises or forward guidance from MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN.

factors.name: CPI, inflation surprises
factors.summary: No major US inflation data in last 24h. Canada CPI soft per 15:28 (RBC notes energy-driven spike offset by base effects), but no US PCE or CPI release to trigger re-pricing. Treasury yield rise appears structural (Fed tightening stance, not fresh data shock).

factors.name: Sector rotation & breadth
factors.summary: Healthcare finding footing while semiconductors slide sharply (Investinglive 14:46). Walmart hits all-time high (15:49), Home Depot beats expectations (15:33), suggesting defensive/consumer resilience. Rotation OUT of mega-cap tech into value/staples.

factors.name: Crypto correlation (risk-on/off proxy)
factors.summary: Bitcoin stays pinned below $77K amid rising US bond yields and oil prices (Cointelegraph 16:00). BTC at 'crucial level of support.' Crypto weakness typically mirrors equities during risk-off; no signs of crypto strength to underpin NQ.

factors.name: VIX, volatility regime
factors.summary: No explicit VIX or VXN data in newsflow, but semiconductor selloff and sector rotation suggest elevated tension. Euronext beat on volatility-driven trading (15:45) implies market chop; not yet panic-level fear.

factors.name: Prior bias history consistency
factors.summary: Three consecutive NEUTRAL5/10 reads (05:00, 08:37, 12:10) suggest market has been range-bound with no clear confirmation. Today's real yield spike + tech selloff + sector rotation marks a FRESH SHIFT toward downside pressure; this is NOT a sustained trend yet.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 faces renewed downside pressure from a structural rise in real yields (30-year Treasury at 5.19%, highest since 2008) and a sharp selloff in mega-cap semiconductors (NVDA -1.48%, AMD -5.71%), signaling duration concerns and demand uncertainty. Sector rotation into defensive names (Walmart ATH, Home Depot beat) and weakness in crypto (BTC pinned below $77K) reflects classic risk-off posture. USD strength (EURUSD at 4-month lows, DXY poised to test 100.00) further headwinds NQ's multinational revenue exposure. However, the move is nascent (PHASE_2) — prior three reads were all NEUTRAL, and no earnings surprise or major economic shock has triggered this; market remains in choppy consolidation with elevated volatility but not yet capitulation.
catalysts_ahead: Fed speakers (Perli mentioned T-Bill purchases adjustable, no major policy shifts signaled)
FOMC meeting (if scheduled within 72h, not explicitly mentioned but typical May calendar)
Mega-cap earnings (AAPL, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN, NVDA dates not in newsflow, but likely imminent post-May 19)
Iran negotiations update (Trump gave Iran until Sunday/early next week to deal; outcome could shift energy/risk sentiment)
CPI or PCE release (no dates specified in newsflow; check calendar for late May)
Semiconductor supply chain updates (TSMC earnings/outlook, Samsung labor strike resolution 15:43)
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) Fed officials signal dovish pivot or rate-cut timeline (would support long-duration tech rally); (2) hyperscalers announce massive capex increases or positive AI demand commentary (would lift NVDA/MSFT/GOOGL); (3) Iran deal struck quickly, oil prices collapse, reducing real yield pressure; (4) major tech earnings beat expectations with strong forward guidance. Also watch for a move below 20,000 support — if broken decisively on volume, downtrend may accelerate; conversely, a retest and hold of resistance near 20,400 + Fed dovish leak could spark a reversal.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 (selloff underway, ~1-2% down from yesterday). Avoid chasing shorts here; wait for retest of 20,400 resistance or a 1-2% bounce into selling. If resistance holds and yields stay elevated, short setup improves. Longs should wait for clear break below 20,000 support *and* follow-through capitulation (panic volume) before attempting reversal entry. Current state = choppy consolidation with downside bias, not yet a tradeable trend.

factors.name: 10Y Treasury Yield Spike
factors.summary: 10-year yield hit 5.19%, highest since pre-2008 financial crisis (per FXStreet, 17:39). Rising real yields compress long-duration tech multiples; Nasdaq components extremely sensitive to this dynamic.

factors.name: Iran Geopolitical Risk / Oil Volatility
factors.summary: Uncertainty over US-Iran military strikes remains unresolved; WTI at $103.20 (+1.16% on day), mortgage rates surged to July 2025 highs. Energy shock driving inflation concerns and bond volatility that pressures growth equities.

factors.name: AI Narrative & Capex Outlook
factors.summary: Google I/O 2026 announcements (Gemini Spark, new models), Anthropic hiring Andrej Karpathy from Tesla, SpaceX IPO prep. AI enthusiasm remains intact despite near-term macro headwinds; positive for mega-cap tech (GOOGL, MSFT, NVDA in NDX).

factors.name: Fed Policy Uncertainty / Warsh Takeover
factors.summary: Kevin Warsh takes Fed helm this week amid pressure to cut rates; Trump signals he'll 'let Warsh do what he wants' (per MarketWatch). High uncertainty on monetary policy direction compounds yield volatility; no clear inflation relief in sight.

factors.name: Dollar Strength
factors.summary: DXY near one-month highs on hawkish Fed expectations and Iran tensions (per FXStreet, 16:23). Stronger USD supports dollar-heavy mega-cap earnings but also reflects risk-off sentiment that typically pressures equities.

factors.name: Session Price Action & Momentum
factors.summary: NAS100 down ~0.40% intra-session after testing support cluster (tested 25,988 per Investinglive, 17:16); bounced but still net negative on day. 4h change +0.23%, 24h change +0.95% suggests choppy, rangebound consolidation post-bounce.

factors.name: Consumer Resilience Signals
factors.summary: Home Depot beat expectations, raised FY outlook, core shopper described as 'resilient' despite high gas prices (per MarketWatch, 15:33). Suggests consumer not yet capitulating; contradicts recession narrative.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Earnings Pipeline
factors.summary: No major NDX mega-cap earnings surprises in this cycle; upcoming: FOMC minutes and crude inventories due Wednesday. Waiting on Nvidia, Microsoft, Google, Amazon calls; earnings guidance will be critical for conviction.

factors.name: Bond Vigilante Pressure
factors.summary: Bloomberg headline: 'Bond vigilantes line up to test Warsh.' Real yields rising rapidly; 30-year Treasury at 5.19% signals sustained long-end weakness and erosion of growth equity valuations.

factors.name: Sector Rotation / AI Capex vs. Macro
factors.summary: AI narrative (Google, Anthropic, Broadcom) offsets macro headwinds, but energy shock and bond rout creating cross-currents. No clear sector leadership emerges from news; risk-on AI vs. risk-off macro in tug-of-war.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 is caught in a macro crossfire: soaring Treasury yields (10Y at 5.19%, highest since pre-2008) and Iran geopolitical uncertainty are compressing long-duration growth valuations, while AI capex enthusiasm (Google I/O, Anthropic, SpaceX IPO narrative) provides some support. Session action shows initial weakness and rebound, typical of PHASE_2 consolidation with no clear directional momentum. Incoming Fed chair Warsh creates policy uncertainty; Wednesday FOMC minutes and crude inventory data are critical decision points. Consumer remains resilient (Home Depot beat), but bond vigilantes are testing the market's ability to sustain high yields without equity capitulation.
catalysts_ahead: FOMC minutes (Wednesday, per MarketWatch 18:00)
Crude oil inventory data (Wednesday, per MarketWatch 18:00)
Mega-cap earnings guidance (NVDA, MSFT, GOOGL, AMZN in coming weeks)
Kevin Warsh Fed confirmation/first policy statement
Iran military action developments (headline risk, intra-day moves)
Treasury yield reaction to inflation data / Fed communications
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if (1) 10Y yield breaks below 4.90% sharply on Fed dovish surprise, re-igniting risk-on rally; (2) Iran conflict resolves peacefully, oil drops, inflation fears ease → Nasdaq re-rates higher; (3) mega-cap earnings guidance comes in stronger than feared, justifying valuations despite high rates. Conversely, further yield spikes above 5.30% or escalation in Iran tensions could trigger sharp selloff below 28500.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in PHASE_2 consolidation post-bounce; wait for retest of either 28600–28700 support or 28950 resistance before committing. Avoid chasing higher into overbought zone; Wednesday FOMC minutes / CPI telegrams could spark directional break. Consider short on resistance retest if yields stay >5.15%; long only on pullback to support with yield stabilization below 5.10%.

factors.name: Treasury Yield Surge
factors.summary: 10-year yield topped 5.19% (highest since pre-2008 financial crisis per Bloomberg), 30-year at 5.19%. Long-duration growth stocks (core Nasdaq holdings) are directly pressured by higher real yields.

factors.name: Geopolitical Risk (Iran)
factors.summary: Conflicting Iran war signals (Trump 'hour away' from strikes vs. Vance 'progress made') create tail risk. Oil inventory draw much larger than expected, pushing energy higher and inflation concerns up—negative for equities, positive for yields.

factors.name: USD Strength
factors.summary: DXY rallies to 1-month highs on hawkish Fed expectations and strong ADP jobs data (42,250 avg/month, strongest since Oct 2025). Strong dollar headwind for Nasdaq mega-caps with heavy international revenue.

factors.name: Real Yields Rising
factors.summary: Bond market 'capitulation' on inflation fears; traders pricing higher rates through 2027 (prediction markets show hike odds rising by July 2027). Real yields are the single biggest drag on Nasdaq's high-multiple, long-duration names.

factors.name: Market Breadth Deterioration
factors.summary: Nasdaq -0.84%, Russell 2000 -1.01%, S&P 500 -0.67% at close; tech and small-cap led declines. QQQ/NQ underperformed broader indices, suggesting growth rotation out.

factors.name: Inflation Signal (Oil Draw)
factors.summary: Crude inventory draw 'much greater than expected' signals demand strength and potential inflation persistence, forcing Fed to remain hawkish—negative for growth equities.

factors.name: Fed Policy Messaging Mixed
factors.summary: Trump says he'll 'let Warsh do what he wants' with rates (potential dovish signal), but market is pricing in hikes, not cuts. Warsh confirmation likely today—watch for hawkish testimony.

factors.name: Mega-Cap Tech Earnings Pipeline
factors.summary: Google I/O announcements (AI models, agents, cheaper enterprise solutions) positive for sentiment, but no material Nvidia/AAPL/MSFT earnings until later. Not a near-term driver.

factors.name: Crypto Correlation
factors.summary: Bitcoin below $77,000 with >$1B ETF outflows; crypto weakness often precedes broader risk-off in Nasdaq. Stablecoin/liquidity concerns (Tether/Circle) add tail risk.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq is in a transition from neutral chop to downside momentum, driven by a confluence of hawkish surprises: 10-year Treasury yields spiking to 16-month highs on oil-driven inflation fears and strong labor data (ADP beat), forcing real yields higher and long-duration growth multiples under pressure. Geopolitical tail risk (Iran strikes remain on the table) and USD strength add headwinds for large-cap tech. The 4h decline of -0.60% and 24h gain of +0.73% suggest a choppy morning into a sell-off afternoon—consistent with a Phase 2 reaction to overnight yield moves. Mega-cap mega-caps (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA) lack near-term catalysts to offset multiple compression.
catalysts_ahead: Fed's Paulson speaks today (2300 GMT / 1900 ET) — watch for hawkish/dovish cues ahead of Warsh confirmation
PBoC rate decision (Asia hours May 20)
Further Iran developments or US military action (intraday risk)
CPI/inflation prints (upcoming week) — high stakes for real yield direction
Mega-cap earnings season (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA guidance) — likely next 2-3 weeks
screening.risk_warning: Bias invalidated if: (1) 10-year yield pulls back below 5.05% on dovish Fed pivot signal (Warsh dovish, or geopolitical de-escalation); (2) Iran deal breakthrough announced → oil drops, yields ease, risk-on resumes; (3) Nasdaq holds above 29000 with relief rally into a bullish close → suggests capitulation bottom being found. If any of these occur, flip to NEUTRAL or BUY.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (downside underway but not yet extended); wait for retest of intraday resistance (29000–29100) or pullback to 28600–28800 support cluster before shorting. Do not chase into a late-day gap down; yield curve remains the dominant driver—monitor 10-year yield for direction confirmation.
